proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the binary patient features, at least one is associated with a higher or lower probability of objective_response than the cohort baseline (~16.9%); we expect feature_035 (the most prevalent strong-looking signal) to be associated with a HIGHER objective_response rate.

analyses.code: for each binary int feature: chi2_contingency(crosstab(feature, objective_response)); rank by p.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate scan of 80 binary features. Strongest signal: feature_035 (n_pos=3624): response rate 0.221 when feature=1 vs 0.165 when feature=0 (diff=+0.056, chi2 p=3.52e-18). Number of features significant at p<0.05: 4; at p<0.001: 1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one continuous feature differs in mean between responders and non-responders; we expect the strongest predictor (feature_092 — appears to be a high-skew biomarker, possibly LDH or %blasts) to be HIGHER in non-responders.
Patient age (feature_078, range 18-92, mean 68) is associated with objective_response; older patients have LOWER response rates.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature) for each continuous feature; rank by p.
analyses.result_summary: Strongest continuous predictor: feature_006 (mean responders=58.233, non-responders=60.309, diff=-2.076, logistic beta=-0.0057, p=0.00e+00). Number of continuous features significant at p<0.05: 8/43.
feature_078 (age): mean responders=68.03, non-responders=67.98, diff=+0.05 years, logistic beta=+0.00040 per year, p=7.18e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective response rate differs across racial groups (feature_005: white/hispanic/black/asian/other); specifically, Black patients have a LOWER response rate than White patients.
Objective response rate differs across insurance groups (feature_087: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured); specifically, Medicaid patients have a LOWER response rate than privately-insured patients.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(feature_005, objective_response)); subset to white/black for pairwise.
analyses.result_summary: Response rates by race: white=0.1728, hispanic=0.1670, black=0.1574, asian=0.1633. Overall chi2 p=9.59e-03. Black vs White pairwise chi2 p=4.00e-03, diff=-0.0154.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(crosstab(feature_087, objective_response)); subset to medicaid/private for pairwise.
analyses.result_summary: Response rates by insurance: medicare=0.1718, private=0.1666, medicaid=0.1687, uninsured=0.1662. Overall chi2 p=5.40e-01. Medicaid vs Private pairwise chi2 p=6.91e-01, diff=+0.0022.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_018 (ordinal 0-10, plausibly stage/line-of-therapy/comorbidity count) are associated with LOWER objective_response (negative slope on logit scale).
Higher values of feature_057 (ordinal 0/1/2, plausibly performance status) are associated with LOWER objective_response.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_018); response rate by level.
analyses.result_summary: feature_018 logistic beta=+0.0042, p=3.40e-01. Response rate at level 0=0.168; at max level (10)=0.190.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_057); response rate by level.
analyses.result_summary: feature_057 logistic beta=-0.3837, p=1.84e-97. Response rate at level 0=0.213; at level 2=0.112.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of objective_response on the 13 top univariate predictors, feature_035 retains a positive, statistically-significant adjusted association with response.
Adjusted for top covariates, feature_018 retains a negative, statistically-significant association with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit (n=50000, 13 predictors). feature_035 adjusted beta=+0.3588, p=2.34e-17 (OR=1.432). Pseudo-R2=0.0189. Top 5 by |z|: feature_057 beta=-0.384 p=7.3e-97, feature_011 beta=-0.040 p=1.1e-34, feature_006 beta=-0.006 p=6.2e-20, feature_035 beta=+0.359 p=2.3e-17, feature_099 beta=+0.117 p=1.1e-06
feature_018 adjusted beta=+0.0046, p=3.06e-01 (OR per unit=1.005).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG-like (feature_018), and other top biomarkers, the disparity for Black patients on objective_response (vs White, the reference) PERSISTS — Black patients have lower adjusted odds of response.
After adjusting for top biomarkers, Medicaid-insured patients still have lower adjusted odds of objective_response than privately-insured patients.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ race + insurance + age + feature_018 + top biomarkers); examine race coefficients.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted race coefficients (reference=asian by alpha order; first dummy dropped): race_black beta=-0.0293 p=6.35e-01; race_hispanic beta=+0.0443 p=4.52e-01; race_white beta=+0.0796 p=1.28e-01.
Adjusted insurance coefficients: ins_medicaid beta=+nan p=nan; ins_uninsured beta=-0.0103 p=8.80e-01; ins_private beta=-0.0197 p=6.00e-01.
